pr.title: Cleanup pointers for Luigi, Marth, Purin, Mewtwo, and CLink
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large asm/data.s cleanup that converted many fighter data-table entries for Luigi, Marth/Mars, Purin, Mewtwo, and CLink from raw hexadecimal embedded strings and raw 0x803xxxxx addresses into labeled data symbols, .asciz strings, and symbolic .4byte references. The PR had no body or review comments, so the intent is inferred from the title and diff: improve readability/searchability of fighter data pointers while preserving the data layout.
what_changed: Replaced many raw pointer constants such as .4byte 0x803D0B00, 0x803D0628, 0x803CFA58, 0x803D0FA0, and 0x803D1498 with symbolic labels such as lbl_803D0B00, lbl_803D0628, lbl_803CFA58, lbl_803D0FA0, and lbl_803D1498.
Decoded blocks of hexadecimal .4byte string data into explicit .asciz labels with .balign 4, especially fighter file/resource names and demo motion-file keys.

Added .global labels for previously anonymous data addresses, including Marth/Mars strings around lbl_803CF820-lbl_803CFA58, Purin strings and tables around lbl_803D02F0-lbl_803D0628, Luigi strings/tables around lbl_803D08A8-lbl_803D0B00, Mewtwo strings/tables around lbl_803D0D80-lbl_803D0FA0, and CLink strings/tables around lbl_803D1240-lbl_803D1498.

Created explicit pointer arrays for decoded string groups, for example lbl_803CFA08 pointing to Marth/Mars demo motion file strings and lbl_803CFA18 pointing to Marth/Mars costume/resource strings; similar patterns appear for Purin, Luigi, Mewtwo, and CLink.
Converted references in higher-level tables such as lbl_803C12E0, lbl_803C1F40, lbl_803C2360, and lbl_803C2468 from address literals to labels where the targets were identified.

Kept some remaining .4byte 0x803D.... values as raw addresses where this PR did not label/convert them, indicating the cleanup was targeted rather than a blanket conversion of every pointer in the nearby tables.
smart_moves: Decoded byte-packed string literals into .asciz form instead of leaving unreadable 32-bit hex words, making resource names directly greppable and reviewable.
Added labels at exact existing addresses and preserved alignment with .balign 4, which is important for not perturbing binary layout in asm data sections.
Changed pointer table entries to symbolic references only after introducing corresponding labels, avoiding magic-address dependencies while keeping the same underlying addresses.
Grouped related fighter asset strings into nearby labeled runs and then introduced pointer arrays over those labels, matching the data's semantic structure without requiring C struct definitions yet.
Preserved internal asset naming as seen in data, e.g. Marth appears as Mars in asset names like PlMs.dat, ftDataMars, and PlyMars5K_Share_joint.
decomp_lessons: When cleaning asm data, first decode obvious ASCII .4byte runs into .asciz labels; this improves future C migration and helps identify table boundaries.

Use labels for 0x803xxxxx data pointers whenever the target address is known, especially in top-level fighter data tables, so later decomp work can reason about relationships instead of magic addresses.
Maintain .balign 4 after .asciz strings in pointer-heavy data sections; alignment preservation is part of the match, not just formatting.
Do not over-convert unknown addresses: leaving some numeric .4byte entries in place is preferable to inventing labels or semantics without evidence.

Fighter asset tables often follow repeated patterns: base file name, ftData key, costume-specific PlXX*.dat names, Ply*5K*_Share_joint, Ply*5K*_Share_matanim_joint, AJ data, and ftDemo*MotionFile* strings.
Internal data names may differ from common character names; examples in this diff include title-level Marth but asset strings using Mars, and Purin/CLink names in file/resource keys.
naming_conventions: Anonymous data labels use address-derived names of the form lbl_803Cxxxx or lbl_803Dxxxx.
Decoded strings preserve original game/resource casing exactly, such as PlMs.dat, ftDataMars, PlPr.dat, ftDataPurin, PlLg.dat, ftDataLuigi, PlMt.dat, ftDataMewtwo, PlCl.dat, and ftDataClink.

Costume/resource file names use short fighter prefixes and color suffixes, e.g. PlMsNr.dat, PlMsRe.dat, PlMsGr.dat, PlMsBk.dat, PlMsWh.dat; PlPrBu.dat, PlPrGr.dat, PlPrYe.dat; PlLgWh.dat, PlLgAq.dat, PlLgPi.dat; PlMtRe.dat, PlMtBu.dat, PlMtGr.dat; PlClRe.dat, PlClBu.dat, PlClWh.dat, PlClBk.dat.

Shared joint asset names follow patterns like PlyMars5K*_Share_joint, PlyMars5K*_Share_matanim_joint, PlyPurin5K*_Share_joint, PlyLuigi5K*_Share_joint, PlyMewtwo5K*_Share_joint, and PlyClink5K*_Share_joint.
Demo motion-file keys follow ftDemoResultMotionFile<Character>, ftDemoIntroMotionFile<Character>, ftDemoEndingMotionFile<Character>, and ftDemoViWaitMotionFile<Character>.
Purin hat joint strings are explicit labels such as PlyPurinReHat_TopN_joint, PlyPurinBuHat_TopN_joint, PlyPurinGrHat_TopN_joint, and PlyPurinYeHat_TopN_joint.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw address constants in .4byte tables with labels only after defining the label at the pointed-to location.
Represent decoded null-terminated strings with .asciz followed by .balign 4 to keep following pointer-table offsets stable.
Use .global for labels that are referenced from other table regions in asm/data.s.
For tables of string pointers, define the strings first, then define a label whose body is a sequence of .4byte lbl_* references ending with NULL when the original table had a null terminator.
Preserve existing function pointer labels and raw constants not part of the cleanup; the diff shows existing func_* and lbl_* entries left intact while adjacent data pointers were improved.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for migration of these labeled asm/data.s fighter tables into C structs or headers.
Find remaining raw .4byte 0x803C/0x803D pointers near Luigi, Marth/Mars, Purin, Mewtwo, and CLink data that were not converted in this cleanup.
Compare this PR's string-table layout against other fighter data cleanup PRs to reuse naming and .asciz/.balign patterns.
Look for later renames of lbl_803CF820-lbl_803D1498 to semantic fighter asset table names.
